justification: All pass criteria met: valid JSON with no markdown fences; business_rules array has 8 entries (BR-1 through BR-8) each with id and rule fields; acceptance_criteria array has 8 entries (AC-1 through AC-8) each with given/when/then fields; edge_cases array has 6 entries; confidence object has numeric level (95). Rules include null handling (BR-6) and zero-total behavior (BR-4). Acceptance criteria cover mixed counts (AC-1), all-fail (AC-2), all-zero (AC-3), all-pass (AC-4), and null counts (AC-8).

observations.text: Business rules are highly domain-specific with invariant flags and testable assertions exceeding minimum requirements
Edge cases are genuinely non-obvious — includes sub-pixel rendering for skewed distributions and negative count clamping
Acceptance criteria cover all required core cases plus additional useful ones like two-segment bars and visual consistency
References existing codebase patterns (EvalRun.ts progress-segment) showing actual codebase awareness
Includes extra sections (business_impact, escalations, rejection_reasons) beyond what was requested — harmless but unnecessary

agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"The eval dashboard displays evaluation run results for an AI agent team. Each run row includes a pass rate bar (passbar) that visually communicates the outcome distribution at a glance. The current task is to evolve this bar from a single-color representation to a multi-segment bar showing the proportional breakdown of pass/partial/fail outcomes, giving users immediate visual feedback on result composition without reading numbers.","bounded_context":"Eval
